# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_snmp_trap (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the SNMP configuration endpoint.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auth_password` (String) Specifies the Authentication protocol password to be used for snmp v3 traps Required for the `snmp_version` matches `v3` and for the `security_level`
- `auth_protocol` (String) Specifies the Authentication protocol to be used for snmp v3 traps Required for the `security_level`
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `community` (String) Specifies the community name for the trap destination.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `destination` (String) Specifies the address for the trap destination. This can be either an IP address or a hostname.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `network` (String) Specifies the name of the trap network. This option is not supported in versions of BIG-IP prior to 12.1.0, and is simply ignored on those versions. The value `default` was removed in BIG-IP version 13.1.0. Specifying this value when configuring a BIG-IP causes the module to stop and report an error. In this case, choose one of the other options, such as `management`.
- `partition` (String) Device partition to manage resources on.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `port` (String) Specifies the port for the trap destination.
- `privacy_password` (String) Specifies the Privacy protocol password to be used for snmp v3 traps Required for the `security_level` matches c(auth-privacy)
- `privacy_protocol` (String) Specifies the Privacy protocol to be used for snmp v3 traps Required for the `security_level` matches c(auth-privacy)
- `security_level` (String) Specifies the port for the trap destination. Required for the `snmp_version` matches `v3`.
- `security_name` (String) Specifies the security name to used for v3 snmp trap Required for the `snmp_version` matches `v3`.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `snmp_version` (String) Specifies to which Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) version the trap destination applies.
- `state` (String) When `present`, ensures the resource exists. When `absent`, ensures the resource does not exist.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
